response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine tooling and IDE-adjacent experience (LabWindows/CVI IDE components at National Instruments, custom test runners and assertion libraries), but the core stack does not match. The role requires deep Java/Kotlin and JVM expertise plus solid Spring ecosystem experience; the resume shows only incidental Java use, no Kotlin, and no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate work. Most recent experience is TypeScript/Node and DLang, so the gap on required qualifications is substantial despite strong craftsmanship signals.
strengths: Direct IDE internals experience building C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI
Built developer tooling from scratch (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts library), showing genuine language-tooling interest
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, plus teaching and workshop experience
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only minor, dated Java exposure — the role's primary languages
No Spring or enterprise JVM framework experience (Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate)
Recent work (TypeScript/Node full-stack, pricing platforms) is far from JVM tooling and enterprise IDE development
